title: OpenThorn vs Lovable
description: Lovable charges $25+/month for credits; OpenThorn is free and you pay your AI provider directly. An honest comparison of pricing, model choice, and code ownership.
answer: OpenThorn is a free BYOK alternative to Lovable. Lovable Pro costs $25/month for roughly 100 credits; OpenThorn charges nothing — you pay your AI provider's raw token rates, typically cents to a few dollars per complete website. The trade-off: Lovable bundles everything into one subscription, while OpenThorn requires bringing your own API key.
intro: Lovable is one of the most popular AI app builders — and a genuinely good product. The fundamental difference is the business model: Lovable resells AI usage as monthly credits, while OpenThorn is BYOK — you bring an API key from any of 18 providers and pay that provider's raw per-token rates. No subscription, no markup.

rows.openthorn: Free — no subscription, no platform fee
rows.competitor: Pro from $25/month (100 credits); Business from $50/month

rows.openthorn: You pay your provider directly at raw per-token rates
rows.competitor: Credits per edit (~0.5–1.2+ credits each), bought from Lovable

rows.openthorn: Yes — built around it (18 providers)
rows.competitor: No

rows.openthorn: Any model from OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Groq, and 12 more
rows.competitor: Models selected by Lovable

rows.openthorn: Full source export, no paywall; deploy anywhere
rows.competitor: GitHub sync available

rows.openthorn: Whole platform is free; you only pay provider API usage
rows.competitor: ~5 credits/day (~30/month)

faqs.question: Is OpenThorn a free alternative to Lovable?
faqs.answer: Yes. The OpenThorn platform is completely free — you connect your own AI provider API key and pay only that provider's per-token rates, typically cents to a few dollars per website. Lovable requires a subscription from $25/month for meaningful usage.

faqs.question: Can I use my own OpenAI or Anthropic API key with Lovable?
faqs.answer: No. As of June 2026 Lovable does not support bring-your-own-key; AI usage is billed in Lovable credits. OpenThorn is built entirely around BYOK with 18 supported providers.

faqs.question: Which is cheaper, OpenThorn or Lovable?
faqs.answer: For most usage, OpenThorn — you pay raw provider token prices with no markup or subscription. A typical website costs cents to a few dollars in API usage versus a $25+/month Lovable subscription. Lovable can be simpler if you prefer one predictable monthly bill.

verdict: Choose Lovable if you want a polished all-in-one subscription and don't want to think about API keys. Choose OpenThorn if you want to pick your own models, pay raw token prices with no subscription, and keep full ownership of the exported code.

title: OpenThorn vs Bolt.new
description: Bolt.new sells AI tokens in monthly packs from $25/month; OpenThorn is free and BYOK — you pay your AI provider directly. Pricing, model choice, and ownership compared.
answer: OpenThorn is a free BYOK alternative to Bolt.new. Bolt's free tier caps at 1M tokens/month and Pro costs $25/month for token packs; OpenThorn charges nothing and you pay your provider's raw per-token rates — typically cents to a few dollars per site — with any of 18 providers and no monthly pack to use up.
intro: Bolt.new (by StackBlitz) is a strong in-browser AI builder. Like Lovable it resells AI usage — you buy token packs from Bolt. OpenThorn takes the BYOK route: connect your own provider key and pay raw per-token rates with no subscription. Bolt's own team also maintains bolt.diy, an open-source BYOK variant — which says something about the demand for this model.

rows.openthorn: Free — no subscription, no platform fee
rows.competitor: Pro $25/month; Teams $30/member/month

rows.openthorn: You pay your provider directly at raw per-token rates
rows.competitor: Token packs from Bolt (~10–13M tokens/month on Pro)

rows.openthorn: Yes — 18 providers
rows.competitor: No (the separate open-source bolt.diy supports it, self-hosted)

rows.openthorn: Any model from OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Groq, and 12 more
rows.competitor: Models selected by Bolt

rows.openthorn: Whole platform is free; you only pay provider API usage
rows.competitor: 1M tokens/month with a 300K daily cap

rows.openthorn: Full source export, no paywall; deploy anywhere
rows.competitor: Download/GitHub available

faqs.question: Is OpenThorn a free alternative to Bolt.new?
faqs.answer: Yes. OpenThorn charges nothing — you connect your own provider API key and pay raw token rates. Bolt.new's free tier is capped at 1M tokens/month (300K/day); meaningful usage needs the $25/month Pro plan.

faqs.question: Can I use my own API key with Bolt.new?
faqs.answer: Not with the hosted Bolt.new — it bills in its own token packs. The open-source bolt.diy project supports BYOK but you must run it yourself. OpenThorn is hosted, free, and BYOK out of the box.

faqs.question: What is the difference between buying Bolt tokens and BYOK?
faqs.answer: Bolt sells you a monthly allotment of tokens it buys from providers. With BYOK you skip the middleman: your key, your provider's prices, your spend limits, and unused budget simply stays unspent — there is no monthly pack to use up.

verdict: Choose Bolt.new for its polished StackBlitz-powered dev environment and a single monthly bill. Choose OpenThorn to pay raw provider prices with no subscription — or bolt.diy if you want open source and are happy self-hosting.

title: OpenThorn vs v0 (Vercel)
description: v0 meters AI usage in credits on plans from $20/month; OpenThorn is free and BYOK. How the two compare on pricing, model choice, scope, and code ownership.
answer: OpenThorn is a free BYOK alternative to v0. v0's free tier includes about $5/month in credits and Premium costs $20/month; OpenThorn charges nothing — you pay your AI provider's raw token rates, typically cents to a few dollars per site, with 18 providers to choose from and no ecosystem lock-in.
intro: v0 by Vercel is excellent at generating React/Next.js UI and ships tight Vercel integration. It bills AI usage in credits metered over tokens, on plans from $20/month. OpenThorn is a free BYOK website builder: bring a key from any of 18 providers, pay raw token rates, and deploy or export wherever you like.

rows.openthorn: Free — no subscription, no platform fee
rows.competitor: Free tier ($5/month credits); Premium $20/month; Team $30/user/month

rows.openthorn: You pay your provider directly at raw per-token rates
rows.competitor: Credits metered over input/output tokens, bought from Vercel

rows.openthorn: Yes — 18 providers
rows.competitor: No

rows.openthorn: Any model from OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Groq, and 12 more
rows.competitor: v0's own model tiers (Mini, Pro, Max)

rows.openthorn: Provider-agnostic; deploy to Netlify or export and host anywhere
rows.competitor: Optimized for Next.js and Vercel deployment

rows.openthorn: Full source export, no paywall
rows.competitor: Code visible/exportable; Git integration

faqs.question: Is OpenThorn a free alternative to v0?
faqs.answer: Yes. OpenThorn's platform is free and you pay your AI provider directly per token. v0's free tier includes about $5/month in credits; sustained use needs Premium at $20/month.

faqs.question: Can I use my own OpenAI or Anthropic key with v0?
faqs.answer: No — v0 bills usage in its own credits as of June 2026. OpenThorn is built around bring-your-own-key with 18 supported providers.

faqs.question: Should I use v0 or OpenThorn?
faqs.answer: If you live in the Vercel/Next.js ecosystem and want best-in-class UI generation with a subscription, v0 is a great fit. If you want a free, provider-agnostic builder where you control the model and pay raw token prices, use OpenThorn.

verdict: v0 is the strongest choice for Next.js teams already on Vercel. OpenThorn is the choice for BYOK: free platform, 18 providers, raw token pricing, and no ecosystem lock-in.

title: OpenThorn vs Framer
description: Framer is a design-first website platform with AI generation, from free to $100/month; OpenThorn is a free BYOK code generator. Pricing, ownership, and which fits when.
answer: OpenThorn and Framer solve different problems. Framer is a hosted design platform (plans from free to $100/month) where AI assists inside its visual editor and your site lives on Framer's hosting. OpenThorn is a free BYOK builder that generates real React source code you own — you pay only your AI provider's token rates, typically cents to a few dollars per site.
intro: Framer is a polished design-first website platform — closer to a visual editor with AI assistance than a code generator. Sites you build there are hosted by Framer and edited in Framer. OpenThorn generates actual React source code from a description, using your own AI provider key: you can deploy it anywhere, edit it in any editor, and you pay raw token rates instead of a platform subscription.

rows.openthorn: Free — no subscription, no platform fee
rows.competitor: Free tier (Framer badge, no custom domain); Basic ~$10/month; Pro ~$30/month; Scale ~$100/month (annual billing)

rows.openthorn: You pay your provider directly at raw per-token rates
rows.competitor: AI features included with plans — no separate metering, but gated by tier

rows.openthorn: Yes — 18 providers
rows.competitor: No

rows.openthorn: Real React source code you fully own
rows.competitor: A site hosted and edited inside Framer's platform

rows.openthorn: Full source export, no paywall; deploy anywhere
rows.competitor: No editable source export — sites live on Framer hosting

rows.openthorn: Developers and founders who want owned, portable code
rows.competitor: Designers who want pixel-level visual control with managed hosting

faqs.question: Is OpenThorn a free alternative to Framer?
faqs.answer: For AI-generated websites, yes — OpenThorn is free and you pay only your AI provider's token rates, typically cents to a few dollars per site. But the products differ: Framer is a visual design platform with managed hosting, while OpenThorn generates portable React source code.

faqs.question: Does Framer give you the website's source code?
faqs.answer: No — Framer sites are built, hosted, and edited inside Framer's platform; there is no editable source export. OpenThorn generates real React code you can export, push to GitHub, and host anywhere.

faqs.question: Should I use Framer or OpenThorn?
faqs.answer: Pick Framer if you are a designer who wants precise visual control and is happy with managed hosting and a subscription. Pick OpenThorn if you want AI to generate code you own outright, with no subscription and per-token costs.

verdict: Framer is the better visual design tool; OpenThorn is the better code generator. If owning portable source code and paying raw token prices matters, choose OpenThorn. If pixel-perfect visual editing with managed hosting matters, choose Framer.

title: OpenThorn vs Durable
description: Durable generates small-business sites in seconds on plans from about $12/month; OpenThorn is a free BYOK builder that generates code you own. An honest comparison.
answer: Durable generates a small-business website in about 30 seconds and bundles CRM, invoicing, and marketing tools on paid plans from roughly $12/month (billed annually). OpenThorn is free and BYOK — you pay your AI provider's raw token rates, typically cents to a few dollars per site, and you own the generated React source code outright.
intro: Durable targets small-business owners who want a website plus business tools (CRM, invoicing, AI marketing) with zero technical involvement — and it is genuinely fast. The trade-offs are ownership and flexibility: Durable sites live on Durable, and AI usage is bundled into the subscription. OpenThorn generates real source code with your own provider key, free, with per-token costs.

rows.openthorn: Free — no subscription, no platform fee
rows.competitor: Free plan (3 pages, subdomain); paid plans from ~$12/month billed annually

rows.openthorn: You pay your provider directly at raw per-token rates
rows.competitor: Included in subscription

rows.openthorn: Yes — 18 providers
rows.competitor: No

rows.openthorn: Real React source code you fully own
rows.competitor: A hosted site plus CRM, invoicing, and marketing tools

rows.openthorn: Full source export, no paywall; deploy anywhere
rows.competitor: No source export — sites stay on Durable's platform

rows.openthorn: Anyone who wants owned, customizable code at token prices
rows.competitor: Non-technical small-business owners who want site + business tools in one bill

faqs.question: Is OpenThorn a free alternative to Durable?
faqs.answer: Yes, for the website itself — OpenThorn is free and a typical site costs cents to a few dollars in provider tokens. Durable's value is the bundled business suite (CRM, invoicing, marketing); if you need those tools, that is what its subscription pays for.

faqs.question: Can I export my website from Durable?
faqs.answer: No — Durable sites are hosted on Durable and there is no source-code export. OpenThorn generates React source you can export, push to GitHub, and host anywhere, including for free on Netlify.

faqs.question: Should I use Durable or OpenThorn?
faqs.answer: Pick Durable if you run a small business, want a site live in minutes with zero technical steps, and value the bundled CRM and invoicing. Pick OpenThorn if you want full ownership of the code, model choice, and per-token pricing instead of a subscription.

verdict: Durable is the fastest path to a small-business site with bundled business tools. OpenThorn gives you actual source code, free, at token prices. Choose based on whether you want a managed business suite or owned, portable code.

title: OpenThorn vs Wix AI
description: Wix includes AI site generation in plans from free to $159/month; OpenThorn is a free BYOK builder that generates React code you own. Pricing and ownership compared.
answer: Wix bundles AI site generation into its regular plans — free with Wix branding, then roughly $17 to $159/month billed annually. OpenThorn is free with no branding: you bring an API key from any of 17 AI providers, pay raw token rates (typically cents to a few dollars per site), and own the generated React source code.
intro: Wix is the incumbent: a mature, hosted website platform with an enormous app market, and its AI generation is included in every plan. The model is classic SaaS — your site lives on Wix and meaningful use (custom domain, no ads) starts at about $17/month. OpenThorn flips that: free platform, your own AI key, raw token pricing, and exportable React source.

rows.openthorn: Free — no subscription, no platform fee
rows.competitor: Free plan (Wix branding, wixsite.com domain); Light ~$17/month; Core ~$29; Business ~$39; Business Elite ~$159 (annual billing)

rows.openthorn: You pay your provider directly at raw per-token rates
rows.competitor: AI features included in all plans

rows.openthorn: Yes — 18 providers
rows.competitor: No

rows.openthorn: Yes — deploy to Netlify or host the exported code anywhere
rows.competitor: No — requires a paid plan

rows.openthorn: Full React source export, no paywall
rows.competitor: No — sites are locked to Wix hosting

rows.openthorn: Provider-agnostic, host-agnostic
rows.competitor: Large app market, e-commerce, bookings — all within Wix

faqs.question: Is OpenThorn a free alternative to Wix's AI website builder?
faqs.answer: Yes. OpenThorn is free with no branding and no platform domain requirement — you pay only your AI provider's token rates, typically cents to a few dollars per site. Wix's free plan carries Wix branding and ads; removing them starts at about $17/month.

faqs.question: Can I move my website off Wix?
faqs.answer: Not meaningfully — Wix sites cannot be exported as portable code; leaving Wix means rebuilding. OpenThorn generates standard React source you can export, push to GitHub, and host on any platform.

faqs.question: Should I use Wix or OpenThorn?
faqs.answer: Pick Wix if you want a mature all-in-one platform with e-commerce, bookings, and an app market, and you accept platform lock-in. Pick OpenThorn if you want AI-generated code you own, free, with your choice of AI model and host.

verdict: Wix is the safe, full-featured incumbent — at the price of lock-in and a subscription. OpenThorn is the open path: free platform, BYOK pricing in cents, and React source you can take anywhere.
